response.justification: Bogdan brings over two decades of engineering experience, current staff-level tenure, and directly relevant work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq — which maps well to the AI-native engineering environment described. His time at Sociomantic demonstrates genuine high-throughput distributed systems exposure (millions of HTTP requests per second, low-latency async I/O). However, Python — listed as a must-have — is entirely absent from his resume and skills list, which is a significant gap. His distributed systems depth is also dated (2011–2013), and the resume lacks explicit evidence of incident response ownership, stakeholder management across functions, or agile ceremony participation. Worth a screen call to probe the Python gap and operational maturity.
strengths: Active hands-on work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq, directly aligned with the AI-native engineering baseline the role requires
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP req/s with async I/O and low-latency design
Broad technical range (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, Kubernetes, event-driven architectures) backed by 20+ years of continuous engineering work at staff level
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume and skills list despite being listed as a must-have technical requirement for the role
High-throughput distributed systems experience is concentrated in 2011–2013; recent work skews toward developer tooling and product engineering rather than large-scale reliability systems
No explicit mention of incident response, on-call ownership, stakeholder management across functions, or agile/scrum participation — all listed as must-have non-technical skills
